steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The relative clause "which ______ the eye's tendency to perceive objects as blue tinted in low-light conditions" defines what the Purkinje effect is. The skill tested is tense: a timeless scientific definition requires simple present tense.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'has been': Present perfect implies the definition is an ongoing or recently completed process rather than a permanent, timeless fact; incorrect tense for a scientific definition. ✗
  • Choice B — 'will have been': Future perfect places the definition in a future timeframe, which is illogical for describing what a phenomenon currently is. ✗
  • Choice C — 'was': Simple past implies the Purkinje effect no longer describes this phenomenon, contradicting the scientific facts presented. ✗
  • Choice D — 'is': Simple present is the standard tense for stating timeless scientific definitions; "which is the eye's tendency..." correctly characterizes the Purkinje effect as a permanent phenomenon. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
